_provenance.source_pdf: Martino, Pat - A System of Tonal Convergence.pdf

systems.name: The 24-Scale Convergence Engine
systems.summary: The central system of the book: a fixed set of 24 scales (organized by harmonic color — major, minor, augmented, dominant, diminished) that surround any target tonal center like a 360-degree compass and 'bombard' it with directed harmonic motion. Each scale is justified by a universal voice-leading rule ensuring its final note lands within a whole or half step of the root, third, or fifth of the target tonality, making every scale a valid convergence vehicle. The system was conceived at the II-V cadence and treats the dominant seventh chord — with its tritone — as the most load-bearing chord type. Practitioners rotate multiple scales in varying orders over a single target chord, deploying them in turnbacks, cadences, and cycles. The governing philosophy is that harmonic fluency arises from understanding tonal gravity rather than from avoiding dissonance.

members.name: Major Color Scales
members.summary: Scales with major harmonic character, including Scale #16 (major with raised 4th / Lydian-like construction).

members.name: Minor Color Scales
members.summary: Scales with minor harmonic character, including Dorian mode (Scale #20) and G Jazz Melodic Minor.

members.name: Dominant Color Scales
members.summary: Scales with dominant seventh harmonic character; central to convergence on the V chord.

members.name: Diminished Color Scales
members.summary: Two diminished scale types: H-W (Scale #4) and W-H (Scale #5), each offering distinct approach colors.

members.name: Augmented Color Scales
members.summary: Scales with augmented harmonic character, completing the five-color spectrum.

members.name: Scale #11 - Descending Tritone Scale
members.summary: The author's personal scale containing an E-Bb tritone; descending tritone-based construction.

members.name: Scale #15 - Half-Diminished / Half-Whole-Tone
members.summary: Bipartite scale: first half yields diminished color, second half whole-tone; E natural functions as leading tone resolving to F.

members.name: Scale #16 - Major with Raised 4th
members.summary: Lydian-like construction beginning on the 7th of the V chord of F major; raised 4th is the major 3rd of the V chord.

members.name: Target Tonal Center
members.summary: The key center at the middle of the 360-degree compass; the gravitational destination all 24 scales converge upon.

members.name: Dominant Seventh Chord
members.summary: The most flexible chord type in the system, hosting the tritone and variants including 7b9, 7#5, 7#9; pivot of convergence at the II-V cadence.

traversal_rules.name: Universal Last-Note Resolution Rule
traversal_rules.summary: Every scale ends with its final note a whole or half step away from a chord tone of the target.

references.quote_excerpt: last note ... only a whole or half step away from the Ist, 3rd, or 5th or root

traversal_rules.name: Free-Order Scale Rotation
traversal_rules.summary: All 24 scales may be connected in any order for convergence.

references.topic: All scales connectable in any order
references.quote_excerpt: All scales in this system may be connected together in different orders for convergence

traversal_rules.name: Tritone as Pivot Interval in II-V Traversal
traversal_rules.summary: The tritone is the central pivot interval of the II-V cadence.

references.quote_excerpt: central interval of harmonic signficance of the 2-5 cadence has been the mysterious tritone

traversal_rules.name: Final Tetrachord as Convergence Bridge
traversal_rules.summary: The last tetrachord of a convergence scale matches/leads into the target key.

references.quote_excerpt: The last tetrachord of this scale is an F major scale.

traversal_rules.name: Modal Re-Entry Traversal
traversal_rules.summary: Convergence scales may begin on any mode of the target key.

references.quote_excerpt: starting on a different mode of it
This scale is simply a dorian mode of the F major

traversal_rules.name: Chord-on-Chord Tritone Approach
traversal_rules.summary: Target chords are approached by a preceding V7 or its tritone-sub.

references.quote_excerpt: Whatever chord you are leading up to ... usually preceded by a five chord with a tritone

modification_rules.name: Scale Color Substitution (Multi-Color on One Chord)
modification_rules.summary: Multiple scale colors are rotated over a single dominant seventh chord.

references.topic: Using many scale colors on one chord
references.quote_excerpt: many different scale color types on one chord (7th)
scales and their chords may be combined ... in several rotas varying their order

modification_rules.name: Dominant Seventh Variant Expansion
modification_rules.summary: The V7 chord hosts variants (7b9, 7#5, 7#9, etc.) expanding convergence capacity.

references.topic: 7th chord as most flexible chord type
references.quote_excerpt: 7th chord ... most flexible of all the chord types. (7th bs, 7b 9, 7th #5, 7th #9)

modification_rules.name: Chord Extraction from Scale for Resolution
modification_rules.summary: Every scale yields its constituent chords for resolution use.

references.topic: Extracting chords from scales for resolution
references.quote_excerpt: Extract all possible chords from all scales for resolution purposes
Each chord represents a member chord sound of one of the modes of the scale

modification_rules.name: Major-3rd Scale Embellishing Minor Chord Fifth
modification_rules.summary: When a scale's major 3rd conflicts with minor key target, embellish the 5th of the minor chord instead.

references.quote_excerpt: Scale #3 has a major 3rd already in it so it has to used as above to embellish the 5th

modification_rules.name: Enharmonic Scale Equivalence / II-Convergence Substitution
modification_rules.summary: Scales sharing pitches with a mode may substitute as II-convergence scales.

references.topic: G Jazz Melodic Minor as II convergence
references.quote_excerpt: G Jazz melodic minor scale has the same notes ... so it may be used as a II convergence scale

modification_rules.name: Chromatic Tone Resolution of Tritone
modification_rules.summary: Chromatic tones resolve the tritone of the 7th chord during convergence.

references.quote_excerpt: using chomatic tones to resolve the tritone

systems.name: Tonal Gravity Framework
systems.summary: Theoretical sub-system establishing the philosophical and perceptual conditions under which the 24-scale engine operates. Every chromatic pitch has a cadential relationship to any key center (the 'tonal gravity law'), and fluency comes from the practitioner's conscious awareness of five governing factors: key center, Vantage Point, Interval Tonics, Resolution, and the artist's confidence. This framework licenses the 'no wrong notes' principle and positions the Circle of Gravities as the navigational tool for chord-on-chord motion. Priming the ear with cadences and projecting oneself aurally to the target tonality are the key preparatory practices. Mistakes are reframed as generative events; consciousness replaces prescriptive rules as the governing principle.

members.name: Key Center
members.summary: The tonal destination at the center of the 360-degree system.

members.name: Vantage Point
members.summary: The chromatic starting position relative to the key center.

members.name: Interval Tonics
members.summary: Pitch relationships defining cadential pull toward the key center.

members.name: Resolution
members.summary: The directed arrival at the tonal destination.

members.name: Artist's Confidence
members.summary: Internalized readiness enabling spontaneous chromatic deployment.

members.name: Circle of Gravities
members.summary: Systematic map of tritone relationships used as navigational tool in Ch. 8.

traversal_rules.name: Ear Priming via Cadences
traversal_rules.summary: Prime the ear with cadences into the target tonality before each scale.

references.quote_excerpt: brainwash your ear with the tonality ... with several cadences into the key

traversal_rules.name: Sound Projection Technique
traversal_rules.summary: Mentally project sound-attention to the target tonality before arrival.

references.quote_excerpt: project yourself sound wise to it already having your attention (sound attention) on it

traversal_rules.name: Tonal Gravity Law - Chromatic Cadential Pull
traversal_rules.summary: Every chromatic pitch exerts cadential pull toward any active key center.

references.quote_excerpt: tonal gravity law is created once you start to play music (in a key)
converginal relationship in a cadential motion sense to this key center

modification_rules.name: No Wrong Notes - Full Chromatic Availability
modification_rules.summary: Given the five governing factors, the entire chromatic scale is at the player's disposal.

references.quote_excerpt: no wrong notes when factors such as fixation on a key center, Vantage Point, Interval Tonics, Resolution are considered

modification_rules.name: Mistake as Generative Event
modification_rules.summary: Errors are reframed as new harmonic fields to be spontaneously filled.

references.quote_excerpt: A mistake is a new field, a new octave.

modification_rules.name: Consciousness Over Prescription
modification_rules.summary: The system increases awareness rather than dictating procedure.

references.quote_excerpt: system does not dictate to the student what to do other than increase his or her consciousness
